title: heuchera parviflora var. puberula
intro: Heuchera parviflora var. puberula, also known as the hairy alumroot, is a perennial herb belonging to the Saxifragaceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. Its health benefits are attributed to its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin, reduce fever, and aid in the treatment of various respiratory issues. Heuchera parviflora var. puberula can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and has been used in traditional herbal remedies for centuries.
